title: error while loading shared libraries: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6: cannot apply additional memory protection after relocation: Permission denied
body:
### Issue Description

I'm using kinoite, distrobox and podman. I wanted to run a quick ubuntu session with rootless podman, but got selinux error.
I've tried the solution in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11109#issuecomment-891808665 and https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#11-changing-the-location-of-the-graphroot-leads-to-permission-denied, but they both didn't work.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. disable setenforce by `sudo setenforce 0`
2. run `podman run --name buntu -it --rm ubuntu:24.04`
3. podman succeeds
4. enable setenforce by `sudo setenforce 1`
5. podman fails
6. ran `semanage` and `restorecon` like explained in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#11-changing-the-location-of-the-graphroot-leads-to-permission-denied
```
sudo semanage fcontext -a -e /var/lib/containers /var/home/scarf/.local/share/containers
sudo restorecon -R -v /var/home/scarf/.local/share/containers/storage
```
7. still doesn't work

### Describe the results you received

```
error while loading shared libraries: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6: cannot apply additional memory protection after relocation: Permission denied
```

```
$ sudo ausearch -m avc -ts recent
time->Sat Sep  6 14:39:07 2025
type=AVC msg=audit(1757137147.307:1285): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=63405 comm="bash" path="/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6" dev="nvme0n1p4" ino=35903413 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c3,c599 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c541,c727 tclass=file permissive=0
```


### Describe the results you expected

podman works with selinux.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

On fedora kinoite 42. [distrobox](https://distrobox.it/) works as intended.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Had the same issue, fixed it via the chcon command mentionned here https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20314

fixed by running `restorecon` forcefully (`-F`)

```sh
$ podman info --format '{{.Store.GraphRoot}}'
/home/scarf/.local/share/containers/storage

$ sudo restorecon -vRF ~/.local/share/containers
$ podman run --name buntu -it --rm ubuntu:24.04
root@b4ae243c7f75:/# 
```

> sudo restorecon -vRF ~/.local/share/containers

`restorecon -R -v $HOME/.local/share/containers`

...(as the non-root user having the problem) worked for me on Fedora 42.
